jokes.text: Oh, my God! You sound just like you do in the movies. Stupid is as stupid does.
jokes.setup: New assistant doing professional celebrity pre-interview call
jokes.punchline: Immediately becomes unprofessional fan, quotes movie lines to Tom Hanks
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - shows Marylou's unprofessionalism through specific, believable behavior

dialogue.speaker: Marylou
dialogue.line: Oh, my God! You sound just like you do in the movies. Stupid is as stupid does.

jokes.text: Remember when you were in bosom buddies and you had that wig on?
jokes.setup: Already being unprofessional with Tom Hanks
jokes.punchline: Continues fangirling with specific embarrassing reference to cross-dressing role
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of cringe, specific reference makes it more authentic

dialogue.speaker: Marylou
dialogue.line: Remember when you were in bosom buddies and you had that wig on?

jokes.text: This is not the cat counter at petco, OK?
jokes.setup: Criticizing Marylou's incompetence
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd comparison to low-stakes retail job
jokes.explanation: Sharp, specific putdown with perfect economy - the Petco reference is unexpectedly specific and funny

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: This is not the cat counter at petco, OK?

jokes.text: Are you crying? Please, don't do that.
jokes.setup: Hank gave Brian criticism about missing poker game
jokes.punchline: Brian starts crying, Hank awkwardly panics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character moment - his discomfort with emotion is authentic to his character

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Are you crying?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Please, don't do that.

jokes.text: His boyfriend, Hank. They've been together for over a year and a half. Oh, my God. I didn't know that.
jokes.setup: Beverly explains Brian's emotional state
jokes.punchline: Hank's shocked reaction reveals his obliviousness to his assistant's personal life
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Hank's self-absorption, sets up later plot

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: His boyfriend, Hank. They've been together for over a year and a half.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, my God. I didn't know that.

jokes.text: Well, that makes one of us.
jokes.setup: Marylou gushes 'It's a dream come true for me to be working on the show'
jokes.punchline: Larry's dry response reveals his dissatisfaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders character moment - cynical, self-deprecating, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, that makes one of us.

jokes.text: God! Kiss ass much?
jokes.setup: Marylou continues being overly enthusiastic
jokes.punchline: Paula's exasperated aside
jokes.explanation: Standard workplace sarcasm, functional but not particularly crafted

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: God! Kiss ass much?

jokes.text: Did you ask if she'd sleep with you? 'Cause then you nailed it.
jokes.setup: Marylou hopes she asked all the questions Larry would ask
jokes.punchline: Paula's cynical take on Larry's true interests
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor revealing Larry's reputation and Paula's awareness of it

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Did you ask if she'd sleep with you? 'Cause then you nailed it.

jokes.text: Mmm, missed a spot. No, on your face.
jokes.setup: Hank asks 'how do I look?' before going on stage
jokes.punchline: Beverly indicates shaving cream on his face with misdirection about location
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with brief misdirection, competent execution

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Mmm, missed a spot. No, on your face.

jokes.text: Um, Larry, I wasn't in that movie.
jokes.setup: Larry confidently compliments Nicolette on 'Mother, May I Sleep with Danger'
jokes.punchline: She politely corrects him - he's completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic talk show nightmare scenario, well-executed cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Nicolette Sheridan
dialogue.line: Um, Larry, I wasn't in that movie.

jokes.text: Well, why don't we take a break, and we'll come right back, and we'll figure out which movie I'm thinking of.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes he's made an error but is live on air
jokes.punchline: His desperate attempt to cover with fake commercial break
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Larry's panic and attempt to maintain professional facade

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, why don't we take a break, and we'll come right back, and we'll figure out which movie I'm thinking of.

jokes.text: I've been off for 3 years. You've gotta remember that.
jokes.setup: Larry's confusion about her career
jokes.punchline: She matter-of-factly explains why he's confused - she hasn't worked
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the awkwardness with realistic celebrity self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Nicolette Sheridan
dialogue.line: I've been off for 3 years. You've gotta remember that.

jokes.text: Did I do something wrong? Yes.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if he did something wrong after obvious on-air mistake
jokes.punchline: Artie's brutally honest one-word answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - brutally direct, economical, character-specific response

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Did I do something wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: Oh, yes. Yes, I watch everything on this magic box.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if Artie saw his on-air mistake
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic reference to television as 'magic box'
jokes.explanation: Good Artie sarcasm, character-appropriate old-school attitude

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, yes. Yes, I watch everything on this magic box.

jokes.text: I just told Nicolette Sheridan that she was great in a movie, it turned out I was talking about Tori Spelling.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his confusion about the wrong notes
jokes.punchline: Reveals he confused A-list actress with notorious B-list actress
jokes.explanation: Good specific celebrity reference that amplifies the embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I just told Nicolette Sheridan that she was great in a movie, it turned out I was talking about Tori Spelling.

jokes.text: Was it my bad breath? Because, um, I ate an avocado today... but avocado's don't give you bad breath.
jokes.setup: Hank trying to figure out why Nicolette seemed uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: His weird logic about avocados and breath, then correcting himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank neurosis - specific, weird, and self-correcting in an awkward way

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Was it my bad breath? Because, um, I ate an avocado today... but avocado's don't give you bad breath.

jokes.text: If people only knew.
jokes.setup: Context of behind-the-scenes chaos vs on-air polish
jokes.punchline: Hank's knowing aside about the show business facade
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on show business, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: If people only knew.

jokes.text: Paris. Paula... I know. I heard.
jokes.setup: Larry says Nicolette was in Rome
jokes.punchline: She corrects to Paris, Paula confirms she knows about the mistake
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier mistake pattern, building the crisis

dialogue.speaker: Nicolette
dialogue.line: Paris.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Paula... I know. I heard.

jokes.text: 'Cause this is Tori Amos' bio. Tori Amos is a singer, not an actor.
jokes.setup: Marylou defending her research after mistakes
jokes.punchline: Reveals she confused Tori Spelling with Tori Amos - even worse error
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - reveals mistake was even worse than thought, perfectly character-appropriate incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Marylou
dialogue.line: 'Cause this is Tori Amos' bio. Tori Amos is a singer, not an actor.

jokes.text: You get back on the horse. You get back on the horse and you go. Go where? Wherever the fucking horse goes.
jokes.setup: Hank gives motivational speech about getting back on the horse
jokes.punchline: Brian's logical question breaks down Hank's metaphor hilariously
jokes.explanation: Excellent deconstruction of cliche motivational advice, perfect structure with unexpected logical question

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You get back on the horse. You get back on the horse and you go.

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Go where?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Wherever the fucking horse goes.

jokes.text: Oh, my go... do you know who you are? Yes I do. Do you know who you are?
jokes.setup: Fan recognizes Hank at gay bar
jokes.punchline: Gordon's unexpected philosophical response to recognition question
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected response that subverts typical celebrity recognition scene

dialogue.speaker: Gordon
dialogue.line: Oh, my go... do you know who you are?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yes I do.

dialogue.speaker: Gordon
dialogue.line: Do you know who you are?

jokes.text: You are very popular in the gay community, Hank. See, I told ya.
jokes.setup: Fan explains why he watches the show
jokes.punchline: Hank takes this as validation rather than seeing the problem it will create
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for later plot complications, shows Hank's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Gordon
dialogue.line: You are very popular in the gay community, Hank.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: See, I told ya.

jokes.text: Staring? Really?
jokes.setup: Hank mentions Gordon was staring
jokes.punchline: Gordon's confused reaction reveals Hank misread the situation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hank's misunderstanding of social cues

dialogue.speaker: Gordon
dialogue.line: Staring? Really?

jokes.text: Sidekick by day, gay caballero by night.
jokes.setup: Gossip column about Hank at gay bars
jokes.punchline: Clever alliterative phrase playing on gay cowboy stereotype
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds like authentic gossip column writing

dialogue.line: Sidekick by day, gay caballero by night.

jokes.text: Well, that's a lie. We never went to mother lode.
jokes.setup: Reading list of gay bars he allegedly visited
jokes.punchline: Only disputes one specific bar, implicitly confirming the others
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank logic - misses the big picture while fixating on irrelevant detail

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, that's a lie. We never went to mother lode.

jokes.text: Yes! Yes! Rayneene, perfect.
jokes.setup: Hank needs to be seen with an attractive woman to counter gay rumors
jokes.punchline: His excited reaction to finding the 'perfect' solution
jokes.explanation: Sets up what will become the episode's biggest punchline - Hank thinks he has the perfect beard

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yes! Yes! Rayneene, perfect.

jokes.text: Rock hudson was gay.
jokes.setup: Hank explains Hollywood's historical practice of hiding gay stars with fake relationships
jokes.punchline: Brian points out the obvious flaw in Hank's example
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Hank's elaborate justification with simple fact

dialogue.line: Rock hudson was gay.

jokes.text: Tell her to wear something very short and very tight.
jokes.setup: Hank wants to be seen with an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: His specific, inappropriate wardrobe demands
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's crudeness and desperation well

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Tell her to wear something very short and very tight.

jokes.text: Well, I'd like my car detailed.

dialogue.speaker: Tim Conway
dialogue.line: Well, I'd like my car detailed.

jokes.text: I, uh, got the trainee, right?
jokes.setup: Tim realizes he's dealing with Marylou

dialogue.speaker: Tim Conway
dialogue.line: I, uh, got the trainee, right?

jokes.text: What is wrong with you? Nicolette sheridan was on the show last night. I know, but she's on my calendar for tonight, too.
jokes.setup: Marylou asking about doing Nicolette Sheridan's interview again
jokes.punchline: Reveals she booked the same guest twice through incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Marylou's incompetence with specific, believable error

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: What is wrong with you? Nicolette sheridan was on the show last night.

dialogue.speaker: Marylou
dialogue.line: I know, but she's on my calendar for tonight, too.

dialogue.speaker: Marylou
dialogue.line: Tied up. And, uh, harriet? Tied up.

dialogue.line: He'll ask me about speed 2! I'll fill in! I'll have some funny stories about sandra, OK?!

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Now my underpants are gonna smell like starbucks.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Um, he's fanning my penis right now.

jokes.text: It's a good thing you don't work at a hardware store, because then it'd be a bad date, as opposed to this where you've dragged me into it.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It's a good thing you don't work at a hardware store, because then it'd be a bad date, as opposed to this where you've dragged me into it.

dialogue.speaker: Tim Conway

jokes.text: I found that if you rode to the outside fence, then it was much safer, 'cause, you know, sometimes they run into each other on the inside there. It's longer. But it's safer, and I think safety, in this particular instance, is pretty good.
jokes.setup: Tim explains his unusual jockey strategy
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic of taking safer but much longer route in horse race
jokes.explanation: Classic Tim Conway absurd logic delivered deadpan, good commitment to ridiculous premise

dialogue.speaker: Tim Conway
dialogue.line: I found that if you rode to the outside fence, then it was much safer, 'cause, you know, sometimes they run into each other on the inside there. It's longer. But it's safer, and I think safety, in this particular instance, is pretty good.

dialogue.speaker: Tim Conway
dialogue.line: Try not to.

jokes.punchline: Elaborately absurd description of keeping him like a pet

dialogue.line: I have him in a little tin hut up here in beverly hills, little monkey strapped to his leg and everything, you know, just to kind of keep him active.

dialogue.speaker: Paula

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, well, it used to be, it's up to 4 bits.

jokes.text: Letterman already does that.
jokes.setup: Paula suggests comedy bit about wrong information
jokes.punchline: Deflation noting competing show already has that concept

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Letterman already does that.

jokes.text: And I said, 'it's a light bulb, and if you come any closer I'll turn it on.'
jokes.setup: Tim explains using fluorescent bulb instead of rifle on guard duty
jokes.punchline: His absurd threat to 'turn on' the light bulb as weapon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic in threatening situation, perfect Tim Conway storytelling

dialogue.speaker: Tim Conway
dialogue.line: And I said, 'it's a light bulb, and if you come any closer I'll turn it on.'

jokes.text: But, guys who had never walked before came running out of that hospital. That's the truth.
jokes.setup: Tim explains accidentally burning down army hospital
jokes.punchline: Dark humor about miraculous recoveries from fire emergency
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect timing, Tim Conway's signature innocent delivery of outrageous content

dialogue.speaker: Tim Conway
dialogue.line: But, guys who had never walked before came running out of that hospital. That's the truth.

jokes.text: I haven't had any sex since the apple dumpling gang, so this is quite a treat.

jokes.punchline: References decades-old Disney movie as last time he had sex

dialogue.line: I haven't had any sex since the apple dumpling gang, so this is quite a treat.

jokes.text: I know rayneene, well, except when I knew rayneene, she was a man.

jokes.punchline: Reveals Rayneene is transgender, destroying Hank's straight performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect devastating punchline that destroys entire episode's setup, expertly timed and character-appropriate for Harvey

dialogue.speaker: Harvey Fierstein
dialogue.line: I know rayneene, well, except when I knew rayneene, she was a man.

jokes.text: Hank, are you OK? Oh, no, I'm not. I'm not OK.
jokes.setup: Harvey's revelation has just destroyed Hank's plan publicly

dialogue.line: Hank, are you OK?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, no, I'm not. I'm not OK.

jokes.text: One of the great moments in television. Talk show, here we come.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: One of the great moments in television. Talk show, here we come.
